evidence: drsam-99657.bubble — ai_confidence_level_os options bTIFK (0–0 exploratory), bTIFO (1–2 exploratory_), bTIFP (3–4 guided), bTIFQ (5–6 direct)
frontend/src/lib/paths/pathsSessionApi.ts — schedulePathSessionCompletion client-side patch via incrementModulesCompletedCount; no adapter route
supabase/ — no edge function for bTIFU trigger found

functional_audit.check: computeAiConfidenceLevel mirrors bTIFU ChangeThing action bTIFa range_contains_point over ai_confidence_level_os bounds; count 5 returns direct (not guided)
onModulesCompletedCountChanged skips when previous === current and patches ai_confidence_level_option_ai_confidence_level_os when modules_completed_count_number changes
PATHS-07 session completion schedules incrementModulesCompletedCount, wiring bTIFU trigger parity after path session complete

critiques.finding: Unit tests exhaustively cover computeAiConfidenceLevel (8 cases, all pass) but onModulesCompletedCountChanged / incrementModulesCompletedCount have no mocked Supabase integration test verifying patch payload or early-return when counts are equal.
critiques.alternative: Add userProfileHooks.test.ts with mocked loadProfileRow/patchOnboardingAndResults asserting confidence slug and modules count are co-patched only when previous !== current.

critiques.finding: modules_completed_count > 6 falls back to direct via computeAiConfidenceLevel default; Bubble option set bTIFQ max is 6 and has no explicit seventh range — behavior is reasonable but undocumented in code beyond the fallback constant.
critiques.alternative: Add a brief comment in resolveAiConfidenceLevel.ts noting counts above bTIFQ max inherit direct, or extend AI_CONFIDENCE_LEVEL_RANGES with an open-ended direct tier if Bubble semantics are clarified.
